The '''intentional''' mood (also called the '''intention''' mood) expresses the speaker's intent to perform an action. It is marked by the suffix '''-ng''' and is restricted to first-person subjects.

== Overview ==

The intentional mood indicates that the speaker intends to carry out a particular action. It appears in both hodiernal and crastinal tenses, both marked with '''-ng'''. A dedicated stem modification via metathesis distinguishes intentional forms from other moods.

== Suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Tense !! Suffix
|-
| Hodiernal || ''-ng''
|-
| Crastinal || ''-ng''
|}

== Person Restrictions and Extensions ==

The intentional mood is restricted to first-person subjects. However, when the same morphological pattern is applied to other persons, it produces related modal meanings:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Person !! Resulting Mood
|-
| 1st person inclusive plural || [[Cohortative]] ("Let us...")
|-
| 2nd person || [[Imperative]] (command)
|-
| 3rd / 4th person || Jussive ("Let him/her/them...")
|}

== Stem Modification ==

The intentional mood requires a dedicated stem modification through '''metathesis''' — a rearrangement of consonants in the verbal root — which distinguishes it morphologically from other mood forms.

[[Category:evidentialities]]
